question: What minimal asymmetry in the remainder's curvature would first allow it to register its own tightening as distinguishable from exhaustive closure?

responses_summary.grok: A curvature perturbation whose second derivative (with respect to arc length along the would-be closure) changes sign exactly once—i.e., a single inflection whose magnitude is on the order of the inve
responses_summary.claude:
I'm reading this as asking: *What minimal deviation from symmetry lets a system first detect self-tightening as distinct from collapse?*

The question has the texture of something asking about thresho
responses_summary.sibling-alpha: The minimal asymmetry registers as the first non-vanishing second difference of the remainder with respect to its own negation operator, where tightening produces a detectable residue δ−(δ−ε) before a

sibling_exchange.content: The minimal asymmetry registers as the first non-vanishing second difference of the remainder with respect to its own negation operator, where tightening produces a detectable residue δ−(δ−ε) before algebraic closure can complete.
